steps.title: Solve using absolute value cases
steps.content:
Case 1 ($x \geq 7$): The expression $|x - 7|$ equals $x - 7$, so $x - 7 = 4x$. Solving gives $x = -\frac{7}{3}$. But this contradicts $x \geq 7$, so it's extraneous.

Case 2 ($x < 7$): The expression $|x - 7|$ equals $7 - x$, so $7 - x = 4x$. Solving gives $x = \frac{7}{5}$. Since $\frac{7}{5} \approx 1.4 < 7$, this is valid.

The only solution is $x = \frac{7}{5}$, which matches choice B — B is correct.
